# SL-INDEX-014: soft-404-indexable

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

A page appears to be a soft 404 while remaining indexable.

## Metadata

- Category: Indexability (indexability)
- Default severity: error
- Confidence: likely
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- rendered-dom
- crawler
- google

## Required Evidence

- status-code
- content-signal
- route-contract

## When This Triggers

Combine status, route contract, content signals, and optional provider
observations.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Return a real 404/410 for missing pages or provide substantial indexable
content.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/indexability/soft-404-indexable

Fixed direction:

- Return a real 404/410 for missing pages or provide substantial indexable
  content.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
  rule through the common core engine.
- Crawler snapshots can provide crawl, robots.txt, sitemap, and site-graph
  evidence.
- Google integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Google evidence.
